After a delightful breakfast, set off on an exciting full-day game drive across the breathtaking Masai Mara Game Reserve. With a packed picnic lunch, venture deep into the wilderness in search of the Big Five lion, elephant, buffalo, leopard, and rhino along with an abundance of other wildlife.

If luck is on your side, you may witness nature’s raw drama unfold before your eyes as predators stealthily stalk their prey. The sweeping plains, dotted with acacia trees and teeming with life, create an unforgettable safari experience. As the sun sets, return to the lodge for a delicious dinner and a restful overnight stay.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Departure Day","day_summary":"

After a hearty breakfast, bid farewell to the untamed beauty of the wilderness as you begin your journey back to Nairobi. Along the way, pause at the breathtaking Rift Valley Viewpoint, where you can soak in the sweeping panoramas of the vast escarpments and rolling landscapes a perfect moment to capture on camera.

As you continue, our Travelxploria representative ensures a smooth and comfortable transfer, reminiscing with you about the unforgettable moments of your safari adventure. Upon arrival in Nairobi, you will be dropped off at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport, where we bid you a warm farewell and wish you a safe flight home.

packages.package_name: Amboseli Safari and Diani Beach Bliss with Travelxploria
packages.overview: With Travelxploria, your Kenyan escape blends wild adventure with serene coastal bliss. Begin at Amboseli National Park, where vast plains stretch beneath the snow-capped majesty of Mount Kilimanjaro and herds of elephants roam freely in the shadow of Africa’s highest peak. Embark on scenic game drives across the open savannah, where you’ll encounter the Big Five, elegant giraffes, and the swift, elusive cheetah—each moment a snapshot of untamed Africa. Then, trade the wilderness for the coast with a luxurious retreat to Diani Beach, where powdery white sands meet the turquoise waves of the Indian Ocean. Whether you're soaking in the sun, enjoying beachfront cocktails, or simply unwinding in pure coastal comfort, Travelxploria ensures your journey ends in perfect harmony—wild at heart, yet utterly at peace.

Spend a full day exploring the breathtaking Amboseli National Park with both morning and afternoon game drives. As Kenya’s second most popular park after the Maasai Mara, Amboseli offers exceptional wildlife viewing, thanks to its open landscapes and sparse vegetation. Nestled at the foot of the majestic Mount Kilimanjaro along the Kenya-Tanzania border, the park is home to the Big Five, along with cheetahs, wild dogs, giraffes, zebras, dik-diks, and many more fascinating species. After an eventful day in the wild, return to your lodge for a delicious dinner and a peaceful overnight stay.

Upon arrival, our representative will be there to welcome you and provide a short briefing before your transfer to a stunning beach hotel along the South Coast at Diani Beach. Take in the breathtaking coastal views as you settle into your accommodation. Enjoy a relaxing evening with a delightful dinner before retreating to your room for a comfortable overnight stay.

packages.overview:
• Arrival at Katunayake Airport, visit Munnewaram and Manawari Hindu Temples in Chillaw, then proceed to Sigiriya for an overnight stay after visiting Dambulla Golden Temple.
  • Climb Sigiriya Rock Fortress, visit a Spice Garden, explore the Temple of the Tooth, and enjoy a cultural show in Kandy.
  • Visit the Temple of the Tooth and Kandy View Point, then journey to Nuwara Eliya, stopping at Ramboda Waterfall and Hanuman Temple, with a boat ride option at Gregory Lake.
  • Travel to Ella, visit the Sita Amman Temple, and explore the Nine Arch Bridge and Ella’s scenic landscapes.
  • Visit the Murugan Temple in Kataragama, a significant Ramayana site, and explore its spiritual surroundings.
  • Stop at Ussangoda Ramayana Site, visit Rumassala Sanjeevani Mountain, explore Galle Dutch Fort, and relax at Hikkaduwa Beach.
  • Enjoy a boat ride on the Hikkaduwa River, visit Pancha Muga Anjaneyer Temple, and explore Colombo with shopping, a city tour, and visits to cultural landmarks.
  • Last-minute shopping or sightseeing in Colombo before departure, concluding the tour with heartfelt gratitude from Supreme Travels.

Kandy, the cultural capital of Sri Lanka, with a fascinating stop at a spice garden in Matale along the way. Learn about Sri Lanka’s world-famous spices, which have been treasured for centuries and remain a celebrated export today.

In the evening, visit the Royal Botanic Gardens in Peradeniya, located just 5.5 km west of Kandy near the banks of the Mahaweli River. Renowned for its lush landscapes and impressive collection of orchids, the garden attracts over a million visitors each year.

At 4:45 PM, experience the spirit of Sri Lanka with a Cultural Dance Show, where traditional music, vibrant costumes, and age-old dance rituals come alive in an enthralling performance.

After the show, proceed to your hotel in Kandy for check-in and relaxation.

Dinner and overnight stay in Kandy.

Begin your scenic journey upcountry to Nuwara Eliya, often referred to as “Little England” for its cool climate and colonial charm. En route, visit the majestic Ramboda Waterfalls, a tea plantation and factory to witness the art of Ceylon tea-making, and the revered Sri Bhakta Hanuman Temple, nestled amidst the misty hills.

Arrive in Nuwara Eliya, perched at 6,200 feet above sea level, where you'll be enchanted by its old-world English charm—quaint cottages, beautifully manicured gardens, and the grand colonial architecture of the Grand Hotel. The city also features a historic golf course and hosts horse racing events, adding to its British countryside vibe.

Visit the Sita Amman Temple, a sacred site believed to be associated with the Ramayana epic, featuring vibrant statues and sculptures depicting the tales of Rama and Sita. Later, stroll around the scenic Gregory Lake, a picturesque reservoir set amidst the rolling tea hills, and unwind at Victoria Park, a beautifully maintained public park established during the colonial era.

After a fulfilling day, check in to your hotel and relax.Dinner and overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya.

After breakfast, begin your scenic journey to Ella, one of Sri Lanka’s most enchanting highland destinations. Along the way, visit the sacred Seetha Amman Temple, located in the village of Seetha Eliya, just 1 km from the Hakgala Botanical Garden. According to the Ramayana epic, this is believed to be the place where Seetha was held captive by King Ravana, and today the temple stands as a place of devotion and historical significance.

Continue onwards to Ella, a serene hill country village nestled among lush greenery, misty mountains, and tea plantations. Known for its cool climate and laid-back charm, Ella is a haven for hikers and nature lovers alike.

Explore the highlights of Ella, including a visit to the iconic Nine Arch Bridge, a marvel of colonial-era railway construction set amidst dense jungle. Embark on a short hike to Little Adam’s Peak for panoramic views of the valley, and enjoy a relaxed Ella city tour, taking in the town’s cozy cafés, markets, and tranquil surroundings.

Dinner and overnight stay in Ella, surrounded by its peaceful, picture-perfect landscapes.

After breakfast in Ella, embark on a scenic drive to Kataragama, a journey of approximately 3 to 4 hours through Sri Lanka’s stunning southern landscapes. The route winds through tea plantations, lush forests, and rolling hills, offering breathtaking views along the way.

En route, make a stop at the enchanting Ravana Waterfall, one of Sri Lanka’s most beautiful and legendary cascades. Tied to the Ramayana epic, this waterfall is not only a natural wonder but also a site of mythological significance.

Upon arrival in Kataragama, visit the revered Murugan Temple, one of the island’s most important spiritual sites. Dedicated to Lord Murugan (also known as Kartikeya), the Hindu god of war and the son of Lord Shiva and Parvati, the temple draws devotees of multiple faiths, making it a powerful symbol of religious harmony.

Dinner and overnight stay in Kataragama.

Hikkaduwa is a charming coastal town on Sri Lanka’s southern shore, renowned for its golden beaches, vibrant coral reefs, and relaxed, beachside vibe. Its crystal-clear waters make it an ideal destination for snorkeling, diving, and soaking up the tropical sunshine.

A special highlight of Hikkaduwa is the chance to interact with baby turtles, which can often be seen along the public beach. These gentle creatures occasionally come close to shore, where locals and visitors alike can feed them small bits of food—a truly heartwarming and unforgettable experience for nature lovers and families.

Dinner and overnight stay in Hikkaduwa.

A special highlight of Hikkaduwa is the chance to interact with baby turtles, which can often be seen along the public beach. These gentle creatures occasionally come close to shore, where locals and visitors alike can feed them small bits of food—a truly heartwarming and unforgettable experience for nature lovers and families.

Dinner and overnight stay in Hikkaduwa.

Welcome to Colombo, the vibrant former capital and the largest metropolis in Sri Lanka. As the country's commercial and entertainment hub, Colombo is alive with modern skyscrapers, upscale restaurants, luxury spas, shopping malls, hotels, and a lively nightlife scene. Its wide, tree-lined boulevards add a unique tropical charm, making it both a bustling city and a serene escape.

Enjoy a leisurely stroll through Galle Face Green, a seaside promenade that offers some of the most breathtaking sunsets on the island. Sip on a cocktail at an open-air terrace as the sky transforms into a brilliant canvas of gold, orange, and purple over the Indian Ocean.

Treat yourself to a delicious lunch at the renowned Ministry of Crab (at your own expense), one of Asia’s top seafood restaurants, known for its fresh, flavorful Sri Lankan lagoon crabs.

After lunch, indulge in a shopping spree. Explore popular malls such as Majestic City, Liberty Plaza, and Crescat Boulevard, where you’ll find garments, books, handicrafts, and leather goods at competitive prices. For a more local experience, dive into the bustling Pettah Bazaar, where you can shop for a variety of items at bargain rates. Don't miss out on tourist favorites like ODEL and Arina, offering a wide range of fashionable apparel and souvenirs.


Check in to your hotel and unwind after a vibrant day in the city.Dinner and overnight stay in Colombo.

On your final day, after breakfast, take the opportunity to enjoy some last-minute shopping or sightseeing in Colombo. As your unforgettable journey through the beauty and culture of Sri Lanka comes to an end, we at Travelxploria would like to extend our heartfelt gratitude for choosing us to be part of your adventure. It has been our pleasure to guide you through the wonders of our island, and we hope you leave with cherished memories.

Our Travelxploria Representative will assist you as you head to the airport for your departure. We wish you a safe and pleasant journey back home. Please do stay in touch, and we look forward to welcoming you again on your next visit to Sri Lanka.

End of the Tour.

Wishing you a safe journey!

packages.overview:
• Arrive in Sri Lanka and travel to Sigiriya, with an enroute visit to Dambulla Golden Temple and Sigiriya Rock Fortress.
  • Drive to Trincomalee, visit Koneswaran Temple, Kanniya Hot Springs, and Marble Beach.
  • Travel to Kandy, visit a spice garden, the Temple of the Tooth, and enjoy a cultural show.
  • Explore Kandy, visit the Kandy View Point, Ramboda Waterfall, Hanuman Temple, and Gregory Lake before heading to Nuwara Eliya.
  • Visit Sita Amman Temple, Nine Arch Bridge, Little Adams Peak, and explore Ella.
  • Travel to Kataragama, visit Ravana Waterfall, and the Murugan Temple.
  • Proceed to Hikkaduwa via Galle Dutch Fort and enjoy the beach and turtle feeding in Hikkaduwa.
  • Take a Madu River safari, visit Colombo’s landmarks and markets.
  • Final sightseeing or shopping in Colombo before departure.

[{"city":"Sigiriya","country":"Sri Lanka","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Airport to Sigiriya ","day_summary":"

Welcome to Sri Lanka!

Greetings from Travelxploria!

Embark on an unforgettable 8-night, 9-day journey to explore the enchanting beauty and vibrant culture of Sri Lanka. Your adventure begins the moment you arrive. Upon arrival at Katunayake Airport, our Travelxploria representative will be there to warmly welcome you and assist you throughout your trip.

Your journey starts with a scenic drive to Sigiriya, a journey of approximately 3 to 3.5 hours through the stunning Sri Lankan countryside. En route, visit the renowned Dambulla Golden Temple, also known as the Dambulla Cave Temple, a UNESCO World Heritage site. This impressive temple complex, one of the largest and best-preserved in Sri Lanka, showcases a beautiful collection of Buddhist art and statues.

Enjoy lunch in Dambulla before continuing on to Sigiriya Rock. Sigiriya, an ancient rock fortress perched 200 meters above the ground, is famous for its breathtaking beauty and historical importance. Built in the 5th century by King Kasyapa, this UNESCO World Heritage site offers visitors a chance to explore the remnants of palaces, frescoes, and stunning water gardens. Don't miss the Lion’s Gate, a grand entrance with massive lion paws carved into stone, and the panoramic views that await at the summit.

In the evening, check in to your hotel in Sigiriya, where you can relax and unwind after your day of exploration. If time allows, enjoy a leisurely stroll around the hotel or take in the serene surroundings.

Overnight stay in Sigiriya.

After enjoying a hearty breakfast, set off for the beautiful coastal city of Trincomalee, rich in history, culture, and scenic beauty. Your first stop is the iconic Koneswaram Temple, perched atop a cliff overlooking the Indian Ocean. This ancient shrine, dedicated to Lord Shiva, is believed to be the place where Ravana once worshipped Shiva to gain his divine powers—offering not just spiritual significance but also breathtaking ocean views.

En route, stop at the Kanniya Hot Springs, a cluster of natural thermal wells with warm waters said to have healing properties. These springs offer a soothing experience, perfect for relaxing and recharging during your journey.

Next, head to the tranquil Marble Beach, known for its soft white sands, crystal-clear waters, and the smooth, marble-like stones that line its shores. Though not directly connected to the Ramayana, this serene setting invites reflection and relaxation, making it an ideal spot to unwind.

In the evening, arrive in Trincomalee and check in to your hotel.Dinner and overnight stay in Trincomalee.

Kandy, the cultural capital of Sri Lanka, with a fascinating stop at a spice garden in Matale along the way. Learn about Sri Lanka’s world-famous spices, which have been treasured for centuries and remain a celebrated export today.

In the evening, visit the Royal Botanic Gardens in Peradeniya, located just 5.5 km west of Kandy near the banks of the Mahaweli River. Renowned for its lush landscapes and impressive collection of orchids, the garden attracts over a million visitors each year.

At 4:45 PM, experience the spirit of Sri Lanka with a Cultural Dance Show, where traditional music, vibrant costumes, and age-old dance rituals come alive in an enthralling performance.

After the show, proceed to your hotel in Kandy for check-in and relaxation.

Dinner and overnight stay in Kandy.

Begin your scenic journey upcountry to Nuwara Eliya, often referred to as “Little England” for its cool climate and colonial charm. En route, visit the majestic Ramboda Waterfalls, a tea plantation and factory to witness the art of Ceylon tea-making, and the revered Sri Bhakta Hanuman Temple, nestled amidst the misty hills.

Arrive in Nuwara Eliya, perched at 6,200 feet above sea level, where you'll be enchanted by its old-world English charm—quaint cottages, beautifully manicured gardens, and the grand colonial architecture of the Grand Hotel. The city also features a historic golf course and hosts horse racing events, adding to its British countryside vibe.

Visit the Sita Amman Temple, a sacred site believed to be associated with the Ramayana epic, featuring vibrant statues and sculptures depicting the tales of Rama and Sita. Later, stroll around the scenic Gregory Lake, a picturesque reservoir set amidst the rolling tea hills, and unwind at Victoria Park, a beautifully maintained public park established during the colonial era.

After a fulfilling day, check in to your hotel and relax.Dinner and overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya.

Begin your scenic journey upcountry to Nuwara Eliya, often referred to as “Little England” for its cool climate and colonial charm. En route, visit the majestic Ramboda Waterfalls, a tea plantation and factory to witness the art of Ceylon tea-making, and the revered Sri Bhakta Hanuman Temple, nestled amidst the misty hills.

Arrive in Nuwara Eliya, perched at 6,200 feet above sea level, where you'll be enchanted by its old-world English charm—quaint cottages, beautifully manicured gardens, and the grand colonial architecture of the Grand Hotel. The city also features a historic golf course and hosts horse racing events, adding to its British countryside vibe.

Visit the Sita Amman Temple, a sacred site believed to be associated with the Ramayana epic, featuring vibrant statues and sculptures depicting the tales of Rama and Sita. Later, stroll around the scenic Gregory Lake, a picturesque reservoir set amidst the rolling tea hills, and unwind at Victoria Park, a beautifully maintained public park established during the colonial era.

After a fulfilling day, check in to your hotel and relax.Dinner and overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya.

After breakfast, begin your scenic journey to Ella, one of Sri Lanka’s most enchanting highland destinations. Along the way, visit the sacred Seetha Amman Temple, located in the village of Seetha Eliya, just 1 km from the Hakgala Botanical Garden. According to the Ramayana epic, this is believed to be the place where Seetha was held captive by King Ravana, and today the temple stands as a place of devotion and historical significance.

Continue onwards to Ella, a serene hill country village nestled among lush greenery, misty mountains, and tea plantations. Known for its cool climate and laid-back charm, Ella is a haven for hikers and nature lovers alike.

Explore the highlights of Ella, including a visit to the iconic Nine Arch Bridge, a marvel of colonial-era railway construction set amidst dense jungle. Embark on a short hike to Little Adam’s Peak for panoramic views of the valley, and enjoy a relaxed Ella city tour, taking in the town’s cozy cafés, markets, and tranquil surroundings.

Dinner and overnight stay in Ella, surrounded by its peaceful, picture-perfect landscapes.

Kataragama","day_summary":"

After breakfast in Ella, embark on a scenic drive to Kataragama, a journey of approximately 3 to 4 hours through Sri Lanka’s stunning southern landscapes. The route winds through tea plantations, lush forests, and rolling hills, offering breathtaking views along the way.

En route, make a stop at the enchanting Ravana Waterfall, one of Sri Lanka’s most beautiful and legendary cascades. Tied to the Ramayana epic, this waterfall is not only a natural wonder but also a site of mythological significance.

Upon arrival in Kataragama, visit the revered Murugan Temple, one of the island’s most important spiritual sites. Dedicated to Lord Murugan (also known as Kartikeya), the Hindu god of war and the son of Lord Shiva and Parvati, the temple draws devotees of multiple faiths, making it a powerful symbol of religious harmony.

Dinner and overnight stay in Kataragama.

Hikkaduwa is a charming coastal town on Sri Lanka’s southern shore, renowned for its golden beaches, vibrant coral reefs, and relaxed, beachside vibe. Its crystal-clear waters make it an ideal destination for snorkeling, diving, and soaking up the tropical sunshine.

A special highlight of Hikkaduwa is the chance to interact with baby turtles, which can often be seen along the public beach. These gentle creatures occasionally come close to shore, where locals and visitors alike can feed them small bits of food—a truly heartwarming and unforgettable experience for nature lovers and families.

Dinner and overnight stay in Hikkaduwa.

Welcome to Colombo, the vibrant former capital and the largest metropolis in Sri Lanka. As the country's commercial and entertainment hub, Colombo is alive with modern skyscrapers, upscale restaurants, luxury spas, shopping malls, hotels, and a lively nightlife scene. Its wide, tree-lined boulevards add a unique tropical charm, making it both a bustling city and a serene escape.

Enjoy a leisurely stroll through Galle Face Green, a seaside promenade that offers some of the most breathtaking sunsets on the island. Sip on a cocktail at an open-air terrace as the sky transforms into a brilliant canvas of gold, orange, and purple over the Indian Ocean.

Treat yourself to a delicious lunch at the renowned Ministry of Crab (at your own expense), one of Asia’s top seafood restaurants, known for its fresh, flavorful Sri Lankan lagoon crabs.

After lunch, indulge in a shopping spree. Explore popular malls such as Majestic City, Liberty Plaza, and Crescat Boulevard, where you’ll find garments, books, handicrafts, and leather goods at competitive prices. For a more local experience, dive into the bustling Pettah Bazaar, where you can shop for a variety of items at bargain rates. Don't miss out on tourist favorites like ODEL and Arina, offering a wide range of fashionable apparel and souvenirs.


Check in to your hotel and unwind after a vibrant day in the city.Dinner and overnight stay in Colombo.

packages.package_name: Timeless Europe A Tour of History Art and Nature
packages.overview:
• Frankfurt, Germany: A dynamic financial hub with a blend of modern skyscrapers and historic architecture. Visit the Römer, St. Bartholomew's Cathedral, and explore the charming old town (Altstadt).

  • Dortmund, Germany: Known for its football culture and industrial heritage, Dortmund offers attractions like the Signal Iduna Park, the German Football Museum, and the Westfalenpark with its stunning views.

  • Hanover, Germany: A city rich in history and culture, home to magnificent gardens like Herrenhausen Gardens, the Lower Saxony State Museum, and a lively arts scene.

  • Aarhus, Denmark: A city where the past meets the future, with attractions like the ARoS Aarhus Art Museum, the Old Town (Den Gamle By), and vibrant waterfront areas.

  • Stavanger, Norway: A coastal city famous for its stunning natural beauty, including the famous Preikestolen (Pulpit Rock). Explore its charming old town and fascinating Viking history.

  • Bergen, Norway: Nestled between mountains and fjords, Bergen offers picturesque streets, the UNESCO World Heritage-listed Bryggen, and access to stunning fjord cruises.

  • Oslo, Norway: Norway’s capital is known for its vibrant cultural scene, including the Viking Ship Museum, the Munch Museum, and the beautiful Oslofjord, offering a perfect mix of urban life and nature.

  • Stockholm, Sweden: A city spread across 14 islands, known for its beautiful architecture, world-class museums like the Vasa Museum, and vibrant district of Gamla Stan (Old Town).

  • Copenhagen, Denmark: Denmark’s capital is a mix of royal history, modern design, and Scandinavian charm. Must-visit attractions include Tivoli Gardens, Nyhavn, and the iconic Little Mermaid statue.

  • Berlin, Germany: A city that blends history and modernity, Berlin is known for its rich cultural heritage, the Berlin Wall remnants, Brandenburg Gate, Museum Island, and a vibrant nightlife scene.

Today, we’ll explore the Rhine and Moselle Valleys, two rivers full of history and dotted with charming villages. We´ll begin with a short cruise on the Rhine, between the villages of Rüdesheim and Sankt Goar, undoubtedly the most picturesque section of the river. Next, we’ll head to the Moselle River, stopping for lunch in Cochem, a picturesque village on the Rhine riverbank overlooked by its imposing castle, which dates back over a thousand years. Afterward, we’ll travel to Cologne, where you´ll have time to visit its impressive Gothic cathedral. Standing at 154 meters tall, it was the tallest building in the world until the late 19th century. You can also enjoy a walk along the Rhine River and through the lively city center before we continue to our hotel in Dortmund.

Today, we’ll embark on a magical journey, reminiscing about our favorite childhood stories and movies as we travel through the towns that inspired the Brothers Grimm, now known as the Fairy Tale Route. Our first stop will be Trendelburg, a picturesque walled village where we’ll visit the large medieval castle and the famous Rapunzel Tower (admission included).


We’ll continue along scenic roads, crossing forests that will remind us of the tale of Hansel and Gretel. Our next stop will be Höxter, a vibrant town where we’ll enjoy lunch and visit Corvey Abbey, an ancient 9th-century monastery and World Heritage Site (admission included). We’ll then make a brief stop in Polle to photograph the exterior ruins of Cinderella’s Castle. Our journey will continue to Hamelin, renowned for the tale of “The Pied Piper of Hamelin”. After following the mouse tracks, we’ll proceed to Hanover, where we’ll have time to explore the city center and take a stroll next to the old town hall.

After breakfast, we’ll head to Hamburg, where we’ll see its lively port surrounding the lake. Following lunch, we’ll travel to Flensburg, a charming seaside village with a marina on the border with Denmark. Our journey will then continue along the Jutland peninsula until we reach Aarhus, Denmark’s second-largest city.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Hanover","to":"AARHUS","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":695,"h_name":"Comwell Aarhus, Dolce by Wyndham","h_country":"Denmark","h_city":"AARHUS","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/AARHUS/Comwell Aarhus, Dolce by Wyndham/1741156562158-unnamed (59).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/AARHUS/Comwell Aarhus, Dolce by Wyndham/1741156562232-2021-04-14.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/AARHUS/Comwell Aarhus, Dolce by Wyndham/1741156562273-unnamed (58).jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Stavanger","country":"Norway","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Towards Stavanger","day_summary":"

We’ll travel to Hirtshals and take a ferry across the North Sea to Norway, embarking on a three-hour voyage to Kristiansand. From there, we’ll continue along Norway’s stunning southern coastline. Our next stop will be the charming little town of Flekkefjord. We’ll then arrive in Stavanger, a picturesque town with a bustling port by the fjord.

Upon leaving the city, we’ll stop at the Swords in Rock monument, a tribute to the Viking era, before continuing our scenic route through tunnels, bridges, and some segments by boat along the fjord coast of Norway. One of these undersea tunnels is the deepest in the world. The two boat trips will make this part of the journey both relaxing and spectacular. We’ll arrive in Bergen at lunchtime and take a cable car up Mount Fløyen for amazing views of the town and the Bergen fjord. Afterwards, you’ll have leisure time to explore the capital city of fjords.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Ferry","from":"Stavanger","to":"Bergen","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":699,"h_name":"Scandic Neptun","h_country":"Norway","h_city":"Bergen","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Bergen/Scandic Neptun/1741157889308-2021-10-16.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Bergen/Scandic Neptun/1741157889416-unnamed (67).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Bergen/Scandic Neptun/1741157889425-unnamed.png\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Oslo","country":"Norway","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Oslo/1742149250639-AdobeStock_67424562-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Oslo/1742149251103-AdobeStock_101002987-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Oslo/1742149251252-AdobeStock_120502702-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Oslo/1742149251569-AdobeStock_329111106-min.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Arrival at Oslo","day_summary":"

Today, we’ll experience more breathtaking landscapes, including Norway’s fjords, glaciers, lakes, and forests. Our journey will take us to Sognefjord, the largest fjord in Norway. Along the way, we’ll stop at the stunning Tvindefossen Waterfall. Then, we’ll embark on a magnificent cruise between Gudvangen and Aurland, enjoying the stunning scenery during this hour-and-a-half voyage. Continuing our adventure, we’ll traverse magnificent snow-covered landscapes and pass through the Aurland Tunnel, the longest in the world. We’ll visit the exterior of Borgund’s 12th-century wooden church, known as Norway’s best-preserved medieval church. Finally, we’ll arrive in Oslo with free time to explore the port, main streets, and the artwork in Frogner Park.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Bergen","to":"Oslo","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":702,"h_name":"SCANDIC HELSFYR","h_country":"Norway","h_city":"Oslo","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Oslo/SCANDIC HELSFYR/1741158205917-unnamed (74).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Oslo/SCANDIC HELSFYR/1741158205993-unnamed (1).png\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Oslo/SCANDIC HELSFYR/1741158206076-unnamed (73).jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Stockholm","country":"Sweden","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Sweden/Stockholm/1742148627486-AdobeStock_120699499-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Sweden/Stockholm/1742148628111-AdobeStock_371412259-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Sweden/Stockholm/1742148628272-AdobeStock_441649606-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Sweden/Stockholm/1742148628407-AdobeStock_652792822-min.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Stockholm Arrival","day_summary":"

We include a panoramic tour of the Norwegian capital with a local guide. This city blends vibrant modern architecture with classical buildings, stunning scenery, and large parks with sculptures. Oslo has been named the European Green Capital. After the tour, you´ll have free time to explore on your own.

Next, we’ll depart for Sweden, passing through landscapes of forests and lakes. Our next stop is Örebro, a historic town by a beautiful lake, with a castle and a charming old town. Enjoy some time for a stroll before we continue to Stockholm, arriving in the evening.

Today we’ll embark on a panoramic tour of this beautiful capital city, built on 14 interconnected islands. We’ll visit Gamla Stan, also known as the “the city between bridges”, the lively old quarter filled with monumental buildings such as the Nobel Prize Museum, located right beside the main square of Stortorget which honors Nobel Prize winners, the Cathedral, and the Royal Palace. Whenever possible, we’ll enjoy the stunning views from the Fjällgatan viewpoint. Afterwards, you’ll have leisure time to explore. We recommend visiting the museums and parks. Optionally, you can visit the City Hall and the Vasa Museum.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Copenhagen","country":"Denmark","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Denmark/Copenhagen/1742148229987-AdobeStock_103454612-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Denmark/Copenhagen/1742148230611-AdobeStock_281390852-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Denmark/Copenhagen/1742148230834-AdobeStock_372343220-min.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Towards Copenhagen","day_summary":"

Leaving Stockholm, we’ll head south. After spending some time in the vibrant coastal town of Helsingborg, we’ll take a ferry from Sweden to Helsingor in Denmark in the early afternoon. You’ll get to enjoy some time in Helsingor, Hamlet’s hometown, where the impressive Kronborg Castle stands out, before we continue our journey to Copenhagen, arriving in the evening.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Stockholm","to":"Copenhagen","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":704,"h_name":"Scandic Sydhavnen","h_country":"Denmark","h_city":"Copenhagen","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Copenhagen/Scandic Sydhavnen/1741158538165-unnamed (78).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Copenhagen/Scandic Sydhavnen/1741158538252-2023-10-12.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Copenhagen/Scandic Sydhavnen/1741158538304-unnamed (79).jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Berlin","country":"Germany","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Germany/Berlin/1741005576038-istockphoto-494161874-612x612.jpg"},{"path":"/Destination/Germany/Berlin/1741005576127-istockphoto-486585530-612x612.jpg"},{"path":"/Destination/Germany/Berlin/1741005576171-istockphoto-907886562-612x612.jpg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Arrival at Berlin","day_summary":"

In the morning, we’ll enjoy a panoramic city tour with a local guide of the Danish capital, known for its exceptional quality of life, commitment to sustainability and rich cultural life. We’ll see The Little Mermaid, Nyhavn harbor with its colorful historic buildings, the exterior of Amalienborg Palace, home to the Danish royal family, the Town Hall Square, and the Tivoli Park area. After that we’ll leave for the south of Denmark. In Gedser we’ll take a two-hour ferry to cross to Germany. We’ll arrive in Warnemünde and continue towards Berlin, arriving at the end of the day.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Copenhagen","to":"Berlin","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":650,"h_name":"Mercure Hotel Berlin Tempelhof","h_country":"Germany","h_city":"Berlin","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Berlin/Mercure Hotel Berlin Tempelhof/1741086329657-unnamed (3).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Berlin/Mercure Hotel Berlin Tempelhof/1741086329737-2024-07-01.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Berlin/Mercure Hotel Berlin Tempelhof/1741086329817-20220531_155750.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Berlin City Tour","day_summary":"

Immerse yourself in the local culture with a guided panoramic tour of Berlin, the captivating capital of Germany. We’ll see the historic center, Museum Island, the Reichstag, the Brandenburg Gate, the shopping streets and the city’s beautiful parks. Our visit will also include stops at the Holocaust Memorial and the Berlin Wall Memorial Museum. After that, enjoy some time in the afternoon.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Departure from Berlin","day_summary":"

After breakfast, our journey will come to an end, leaving you with wonderful lasting memories.

packages.package_name: From Frankfurt to Copenhagen: A Pan-European Odyssey
packages.overview:
• Frankfurt – A dynamic blend of modern skyscrapers and historic charm welcomes you to this vibrant German metropolis.
  • Rhine River Cruise – Sail through fairy-tale landscapes, passing medieval castles and lush vineyards along the picturesque riverbanks.
  • Cochem Castle – A majestic fortress perched above the Moselle River, offering breathtaking views and medieval charm.
  • Cologne Cathedral – Marvel at this towering Gothic masterpiece, once the world’s tallest building, dominating the city skyline.
  • Fairy Tale Route – Wander through storybook villages, visiting Rapunzel’s Tower and the legendary Pied Piper’s Hamelin.
  • Hamburg – Discover Germany’s lively port city, with its striking architecture, bustling waterfront, and maritime heritage.
  • Stavanger – Stroll through this picturesque Norwegian town, renowned for its fjordside charm and Viking history.
  • Bergen & Fløyen Viewpoint – Ride the cable car for panoramic views of Norway’s stunning fjords and colorful harbor.
  • Sognefjord Cruise – Glide through Norway’s largest and most spectacular fjord, surrounded by majestic mountains and waterfalls.
  • Oslo & Frogner Park – Explore Norway’s stylish capital, home to grand palaces, modern architecture, and Vigeland’s iconic sculptures.
  • Stockholm & Gamla Stan – Discover Sweden’s enchanting capital, built on 14 islands, with cobbled streets and royal grandeur.
  • Helsingor & Kronborg Castle – Step into Shakespeare’s world at Hamlet’s legendary castle on the Danish coastline.
  • Copenhagen & Nyhavn – Experience Denmark’s capital, where colorful harbor houses and royal landmarks exude Nordic charm.

Our journey will then continue along the Jutland peninsula until we reach Aarhus, Denmark’s second-largest city. 

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Hanover","to":"AARHUS","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":695,"h_name":"Comwell Aarhus, Dolce by Wyndham","h_country":"Denmark","h_city":"AARHUS","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/AARHUS/Comwell Aarhus, Dolce by Wyndham/1741156562158-unnamed (59).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/AARHUS/Comwell Aarhus, Dolce by Wyndham/1741156562232-2021-04-14.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/AARHUS/Comwell Aarhus, Dolce by Wyndham/1741156562273-unnamed (58).jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Stavanger","country":"Norway","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Aarhus Departure & Arrival at Stavanger","day_summary":"

We’ll travel to Hirtshals and take a ferry across the North Sea to Norway, embarking on a three-hour voyage to Kristiansand. From there, we’ll continue along Norway’s stunning southern coastline. Our next stop will be the charming little town of Flekkefjord. We’ll then arrive in Stavanger, a picturesque town with a bustling port by the fjord.

Upon leaving the city, we’ll stop at the Swords in Rock monument, a tribute to the Viking era, before continuing our scenic route through tunnels, bridges, and some segments by boat along the fjord coast of Norway. One of these undersea tunnels is the deepest in the world. The two boat trips will make this part of the journey both relaxing and spectacular. We’ll arrive in Bergen at lunchtime and take a cable car up Mount Fløyen for amazing views of the town and the Bergen fjord. Afterwards, you’ll have leisure time to explore the capital city of fjords.

After a scenic journey through the heart of Norway, you’ll bid farewell to the charming city of Bergen, known for its colorful harbor and fjord-side beauty. As you depart, soak in the last glimpses of the mountains and coastal landscapes that define western Norway. The route to Oslo offers stunning views of lush valleys, deep fjords, and picturesque countryside. Upon arrival in Oslo, the vibrant capital welcomes you with its modern architecture, historic landmarks, and cultural richness. This transition marks the perfect blend of Norway’s natural wonders and urban elegance, offering a complete Nordic experience.

We include a panoramic tour of the Norwegian capital with a local guide. This city blends vibrant modern architecture with classical buildings, stunning scenery, and large parks with sculptures. Oslo has been named the European Green Capital. After the tour, you´ll have free time to explore on your own.


Next, we’ll depart for Sweden, passing through landscapes of forests and lakes. Our next stop is Örebro, a historic town by a beautiful lake, with a castle and a charming old town. Enjoy some time for a stroll before we continue to Stockholm, arriving in the evening.

​Today we’ll embark on a panoramic tour of this beautiful capital city, built on 14 interconnected islands. We’ll visit Gamla Stan, also known as the “the city between bridges”, the lively old quarter filled with monumental buildings such as the Nobel Prize Museum, located right beside the main square of Stortorget which honors Nobel Prize winners, the Cathedral, and the Royal Palace. Whenever possible, we’ll enjoy the stunning views from the Fjällgatan viewpoint. Afterwards, you’ll have leisure time to explore. We recommend visiting the museums and parks. Optionally, you can visit the City Hall and the Vasa Museum.


​

Vietnam Tour from 11th Feb '25 to 20th Feb'25.

Vietnam tour was dream for us for long times. At last we could fulfill our dream with precise guidance of our tour operator Travelxploria.

We started our tour from Kolkata to Ho Chi Minh City (southern Vietnam) to Da Nang (Central Vietnam) and ended up at Hanoi (Northern Vietnam) to Kolkata, covering Cu Chi tunnels tour, Mackong Delta tour, Hop On Hop Off Ho Chi Minh City Bus tour, Marble Mountain tour, Hoi An Lantern Boat ride, Ba Na hill Golden Bridge, French Village tour, Da Nang Dragon Bridge Fire Show, Halong Bay Cruise night stay, Sampan Boat ride, Trung Trang Cave tour, Ninh Binh Ancient Capital tour, Tam Coc Boat ride, Hanoi Cycle Rickshaw city tour etc.

We have been accomodated in premium Hotels and Cruise and moreover liasoning of the tour itinerary in skill hands by our tour operator Travelxploria made the tour Hassle Free, Memorable, Fascinating and Mind Blowing.

Special thanks to CEO of Travelxploria, Ms.Riya Madam, tour cordinator Ms. Jenny from Vietnam and Mr. Bikram Mishra for their experienced organising skill made our tour remarkable.
We wish to travel with Travelxploria in ensuing days for exploring other destinations.

Apart from these two things we enjoyed the entire trip. Thanks a lot to Mr. Sanjib for making our trip so smooth n happening. Thanks to TravelXploria entire team for their cooperation. Looking forward for more ...

overview:
Together We Wander: Community & Group Adventures with Travelxploria

Imagine an expedition where laughter echoes from emerald rice terraces to mist-clad mountain trails—an adventure designed for communities, clubs, corporate teams, or any group seeking shared discovery. As your Best Travel Agency, Best Tour Operator, and Best International Tour Operator, Travelxploria invites you to explore a world of possibilities.

Picture your group navigating the bustling floating markets of Thailand, then unwinding beneath the luminous Supertrees of Singapore—all arranged with seamless, skip-the-line perks. Perhaps you’ll trek through tea-fringed highlands in Malaysia, dive into Bali’s surf-kissed shores, or chase golden sunsets along Sri Lanka’s southern coast.

The desert dunes of Dubai await adrenaline-charged dune buggies, while the monasteries of Bhutan offer serene mountaintop retreats. Europe’s storybook villages beckon with castle-perched panoramas, and the mighty Himalayas call from Nepal, Kashmir, Uttarakhand, and Himachal Pradesh, where ancient trails reveal hidden waterfalls and valley-side villages. Round out your journey with backwater cruises in Kerala, drifting past swaying palms and spice-scented farms.

This is our Package—a curated tapestry of destinations, yet only the beginning of your collective odyssey. Whether you’re a trekking club, yoga community, alumni association, or corporate team, Travelxploria’s Best Tour Operator expertise adapts every stop to your group’s rhythm: private guides fluent in your interests, flexible accommodations from homestays to luxury lodges, and bespoke themes—from culinary workshops to conservation volunteering.

Looking for a touch of romance or family-friendly flair within your group? We seamlessly integrate our Romantic Package, Couple Package, Best Honeymoon Package, or Best Family Package options so every subgroup finds its perfect moment.

Because at Travelxploria, the world is your classroom, playground, and sanctuary. Your group’s story can stretch across continents—or delve deeper into a single region. These twelve destinations are invitations, not limits: your next chapter could unfold on African savannahs, South American peaks, or Arctic auroras.

Gather your community, share the wonder, and let Travelxploria—your Best Travel Agency, Best Tour Operator, and Best International Tour Operator—craft an unforgettable group tapestry. Book your Group Package today, and discover just how far together you can go.
